CustomFunction.name: getScriptEnvironment
IsRunningOnServer
CustomFunction.Calculation: PatternCount ( Get ( ApplicationVersion ) ; "Server" )

CustomFunction.name: Log.Data
Log.Entry
Log.Reset
_error_FAILED_CONDITION
_error_INVALID_CONTEXT
_error_INVALID_JSON
CustomFunction.Calculation: //Returns a constant value "INVALID_JSON"

CustomFunction.name: _error_INVALID_PARAM
CustomFunction.Calculation: //Returns a constant value "INVALID_PARAM"

CustomFunction.name: _error_MALFORMED_ERROR_OBJECT
_error_MISSING_REQUIRED_PARAM
_error_MISSING_REQUIRED_VAR
_error_MULTIPLE_RECORDS_FOUND
CustomFunction.Calculation: //Returns a constant value "MULTIPLE_RECORDS_FOUND"

CustomFunction.name: _error_NO_RECORDS_FOUND
CustomFunction.Calculation: //Returns a constant value "NO_RECORDS_FOUND"

CustomFunction.name: _error_UNEXPECTED
_z_________________
CustomFunction.Calculation: // Just a separator CF to separate constant custom functions from the rest of the custom functions

CustomFunction.name: error.Config
CustomFunction.Calculation:
/* SIGNATURE: error.Config
PARAMETERS: none
HISTORY: CREATED on 2024-10-13 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> MODIFIED on 2024-10-29 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> to rename this function's param from config_property to property MODIFIED on 2024-10-30 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> to add a 4th config property, "default_trace_element" MODIFIED on 2024-10-31 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> Added the following 2 properties: "include_description" and "include_script_param", and thus the properties "description" and "script.parameter" optional in error trace elements. Also made the "state" property not just configurable, but optional to include in error trace elements MODIFIED on 2024-11-07 by Marcelo Piñeyro - split former #Error.Config function into 2 different functions, error.Config and error.PrivateGetConfig, so as to separate specifying the config options from getting the values for those options MODIFIED on YYYY-MM-DD by FristName LastName <email_address> -
PURPOSE: To store in a single place all the developer-configurable options in the clew error trapping and handling technique
RESULT: A json object that captures all of the developer-configurable options in the clew error trapping and handling technique
ERRORS: n/a
NOTES: The asumed error handling convention in scripts that use this function and the clew technique is to use a single plass loop - which is conceptually similar to a try block in other platforms. When an error occurs, the loop is exited, and a local variable is set to a json error object (an errorTrace). To avoid direct references to that variable, so we can easily change that variable's name at any time, we use a combination of this function, error.PrivateSetTrace, and error.GetTrace to define the name of the variable, instantiate it, and read its contents.
DEPENDENCIES: None */ Let( config = JSONSetElement( "{}"
/************* DEVELOPERS: CONFIGURE CLEW OPTIONS BY EDITING JSON OBJECT PROPERTIES BELOW ****************/ /************* Do NOT change the property name nor the type, ONLY change the value ****************/ ; [ "default_trace_element" ; "first" ; JSONString ] // This property determines what error trace element the following functions operate on: error.GetCode, error.GetHint, and error.GetScriptName. If this config property is set to "first", the afore-mentioned functions will extract the corresponding property from the first element in the trace array. If this config property is set to "last", the functions in question will operate on the last element in the trace ; [ "error_trace_order" ; "caller_script_first" ; JSONString ] // The 2 valid values are "caller_script_first" & "caller_script_last". If you choose "caller_script_first", the first element in the trace array is the top-most script in a script sequence, and the last element is the bottom-most script (the script in which the original error occurred). If you choose "caller_script_last", the inverse is true ; [ "error_var_name" ; "$" & "clew.ERROR" ; JSONString ] // This needs to be a local variable; modify only the expression to the right of the ampersand. This is the name of the variable in which the error trace will be stored in any given script ; [ "include_description" ; True ; JSONBoolean ] // If you want the "description" property included in each trace element, set it to true, else set it to false ; [ "include_script_parameter" ; True ; JSONBoolean ] // If you want the parameter passed into each script to be captured in the script.parameter property included in each trace element, set it to true, else set it to false // You may modify the "state" object as you wish, so long as it's still valid json. If you do NOT want to include a state property in the trace elements, set state to an empty string, as in: // ; [ "state" ; "" ; JSONString ] ; [ "state.accountName" ; Get ( AccountName ) ; JSONString ] ; [ "state.applicationVersion" ; Get ( ApplicationVersion ) ; JSONString ] ; [ "state.layoutName" ; Get ( LayoutName ) ; JSONString ] ; [ "state.recordOpenCount" ; Get ( RecordOpenCount ) ; JSONNumber ] /************* END OF SECTION WHERE DEVELOPERS CAN SET CONFIG OPTIONS ****************/ ); config )

CustomFunction.name: error.CreateVarsFromKeys
CustomFunction.Calculation:
/* SIGNATURE: error.CreateVarsFromKeys ( json )
PARAMETERS: json - a json object namespace - a namespace or prefix to add to the beginning of the name of the local variables created by this function
HISTORY: CREATED on 2021-03-30 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> MODIFIED on 2023-01-12 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> added namespace param MODIFIED on 2024-09-24 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> edited documentation MODIFIED on 2024-11-06 by Marcelo Piñeyro - replaced "#Error." prefix with "error." in the function's name MODIFIED on YYYY-MM-DD by FristName LastName <email_address> -
PURPOSE: To create a local variable for each root level key received in the json passed as a parameter
RESULT: false if no error occurs; if an error occurs it returns true, and sets a local variable to a json errorTrace as generated by Error.Throw
ERRORS: n/a
NOTES: The convention assumed for parameter parsing in scripts when using the #Error suite of custom functions (aka the clew technique) is that this custom function is called in an "Exit Loop If" script step at the beginning of a single pass loop (assuming the script accepts any parameters)
DEPENDENCIES: Requires other custom functions. Requires v16 or later FIleMaker client. Pre-16 clients will return "?". */ Let( x = json.CreateVars ( json ; namespace ) ; If( x = 0 ; False ; error.Throw ( x ; "The CreateVarsFromKeys custom function failed to parse the parameter passed to the script" ) ) )

CustomFunction.name: error.DeleteTrace
CustomFunction.Calculation:
/* SIGNATURE: error.DeleteTrace
PARAMETERS: none
HISTORY: CREATED on 2024-09-16 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> MODIFIED on 2024-10-31 by Marcelo Piñeyro - removed errorTrace param from this function MODIFIED on 2024-11-06 by Marcelo Piñeyro - changed name from #Error.Clear to error.ClearTrace MODIFIED on 2024-11-07 by Marcelo Piñeyro - changed name from error.ClearTrace to error.DeleteTrace, so that error.Config sorts to the top when custom functions are sorted alphabetically MODIFIED on YYYY-MM-DD by FristName LastName <email_address> -
PURPOSE: To clear the content of the local variable that is used to store any "thrown" errors, i.e. to clear the clew trace
RESULT: none
ERRORS: n/a
NOTES: The "clew" error trapping and handling technique utilizes a local variable to store any errors "thrown". There are times when we "catch" such errors and want to then clear the error trace. That is the purpose of this function, to clear the variable that stores the error trace.
DEPENDENCIES: References other clew functions. It acts upon a local variable specified in the Config function */ error.PrivateSetTrace( "" )

CustomFunction.name: error.GetCode
CustomFunction.Calculation:
/* SIGNATURE: error.GetCode
PARAMETERS: none
HISTORY: CREATED on 2021-05-17 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> MODIFIED on 2021-10-27 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> MODIFIED on 2023-03-05 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> refactored to conform to new errorTrace json schema MODIFIED on 2024-09-24 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> changed name from #Error.ExtractErrorCode to #Error.GetCode, and edited documentation MODIFIED on 2024-10-30 by Marcelo Piñeyro <mpineyro@soliantconsulting.com> removed former errorTrace param, and modified implementation to point to the errorTrace stored in the variable specified in #Error.Config instead, and furthermore to extract the error code from the trace element also specified in #Error.Config, i.e. from the first or last element MODIFIED on 2024-11-06 by Marcelo Piñeyro - replaced "#Error." prefix with "error." in the function's name
PURPOSE: To extract the error code for the error trace element specified in the Config function, i.e. the first or the last element in the trace array
RESULT: The value of the error code property in the specified trace element
ERRORS: Returns "? Invalid default_trace_element in config" if the default_trace_element in the Config function is empty or if it's set to an invalid value. If it can't parse the specified trace element, it returns the error returned by JSONGetElement.
NOTES: The implementation of this function is very similar to that of some the other error.Get functions. I could create a new function to extract that logic into that function. Whereas DRYness is desirable, adding unnecessary new functions is undesirable. Ugh, tradeoffs. For now I'm choosing to have less clew functions.
DEPENDENCIES: Requires v16 or later FIleMaker client. Pre-16 clients will return "?". */ Let([ trace_array = JSONGetElement( error.GetTrace ; "errorTrace" ); trace_array_length = ValueCount( JSONListKeys( trace_array ; "" ) ); index_term = error.PrivateGetConfig( "default_trace_element" ); index = Case( index_term = "first"; 0; index_term = "last"; trace_array_length - 1; "? Invalid default_trace_element in config" ) ];
JSONGetElement( trace_array ; "[" & index & "].code" ) )

CustomFunction.name: error.GetDescription
error.GetHint
error.GetScriptName
error.GetTrace
CustomFunction.Calculation: Evaluate( error.PrivateGetConfig( "error_var_name" ) )

CustomFunction.name: error.InSubscript
error.InSubscriptThrow
error.PrivateBuildObject
error.PrivateGetConfig
error.PrivateSetTrace
error.Throw
error.ThrowIf
error.ThrowIfLast
error.ThrowIfMissingParam
error.ThrowIfMissingVar
error.ThrowIfWrongContext
error.WasThrown
CustomFunction.Calculation: not IsEmpty( error.GetTrace )

CustomFunction.name: json.ArrayAddToFront
json.CreateVars
json.IsErrorTrace
json.IsValid
CustomFunction.Calculation: Left( JSONGetElement ( json ; "doesnotmatterwhatishere" ); 3 ) <>"? *"
